paragraph: 6) To avoid the disadvantage of too frequent changes of textbooks and the harmful consequences resulting from this for students, it is desirable: a) that the number of textbooks approved by the official authority be limited per subject and per grade; b) that the approval granted be valid for a sufficiently long period; c) that the number of textbooks used in the same grade not be too high, particularly in primary education; d) that too many changes in the text from one edition to another of the same textbook not be allowed unless justified by sufficient reasons; e) that efforts be made, as far as possible, to ensure collaboration between local educational authorities so that students who change schools are not obliged to acquire new textbooks each time; f) that the freedom left to the teacher to choose the textbook to be used, according to their pedagogical views or preferences, be moderated by the approval or ratification of qualified Councils;

It also includes implementation directives (teaching methods, instructional time), aligning with 'Curriculum implementation'. 'Curriculum evaluation and reform' is excluded as there's no mention of assessment or revision. 'Design and development' is less fitting since the focus is on content integration rather than curriculum design.
low_level_thinking.Policy theme: The paragraph explicitly mandates hygiene education across all educational levels, aligning with 'Curriculum and pedagogy policies' as it addresses subject content and teaching requirements. 'Health and well-being policies' is relevant due to the focus on hygiene, a health-related topic. Other categories like 'Governance' or 'Financing' are not mentioned, and the text does not address emergencies or technology.

TITLE: L'ENSEIGNEMENT DE L'HYGIÈNE DANS LES ÉCOLES PRIMAIRES ET SECONDAIRES
paragraph: 2) That the teaching of hygiene have an essentially practical character at the elementary levels where, without overloading the curriculum, it can be provided daily through various aspects of school life and primarily through the formation of habits of cleanliness, conduct, and social graces;

low_level_thinking.Education level: The paragraph specifies 'elementary levels' which aligns with ISCED 1 (Primary education). The mention of daily practical hygiene teaching through school life and habit formation supports primary education focus. Other categories like pre-primary (ISCED 02) or secondary (ISCED 2-3) are excluded as the text does not mention preschool or secondary-specific aspects.
low_level_thinking.Curriculum: The paragraph emphasizes hygiene education's practical focus and integration into daily school activities, aligning with 'Content and learning areas'. It mentions daily provision without overloading the curriculum, supporting 'Organization and instructional time'. 'Curriculum implementation' is less direct as the text focuses on content and scheduling rather than delivery methods. 'Other curriculum-related issue' is avoided due to specificity.
low_level_thinking.Cross-cutting themes & skills: The paragraph emphasizes habit formation in cleanliness, conduct, and social graces, aligning with life skills and 21st century competencies. Health and well-being education is directly addressed through hygiene teaching. Other categories like citizenship or digital literacy lack explicit mention in the text.
